title: nephelium lappaceum var. pallens
intro: Nephelium lappaceum var. pallens, also known as the Rambai tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. This tree is native to Southeast Asia and is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a compound called rambazan, a type of tannin. The medicinal properties of Nephelium lappaceum var. pallens are believed to aid in treating various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, while also providing potential health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. In traditional medicine, the seeds are often prepared as a decoction or infused in hot water to create herbal remedies.
